channel.title: decisions on meanderingexile
channel.description: Recent content in decisions on meanderingexile

item.title: Uncertainty and Risk
item.description: The fundamental difference is pretty simple: when risk kills you, you see it coming. When uncertainty kills you, you don’t. This is Knight’s distinction of risk vs. uncertainty from 1921. In risk the outcome is uncertain, but the probability distribution is known, while in uncertainty both the outcomes and their distribution are unknown. Most of what we’d call risk in day-to-day life is actually uncertainty. We don’t know the probability distributions for things that threaten us.
